sections.heading: Sector 1
sections.body:
Approach in 8th gear with DRS wide open. Brake just after the 100m board, down to 2nd gear. Turn 1 is tight — brake in a straight line and hug the inside curb without mounting it. You will immediately flick left for turn 2, so stay in tight and use minimal steering to keep traction. Do not get greedy with the throttle — wheelspin is easy to fall into here, especially at a race start.

Accelerate hard out of turn 2 and ride the full outside kerb. DRS opens as you climb through turn 3. Stay smooth on the throttle to avoid unsettling the rear and aim for maximum speed as you head into the next straight.

sections.heading: Sector 2
sections.body:
Brake at the 100m board, down to 3rd gear. Cut a good chunk of the inside kerb, but not so much that you unsettle the car. Let the car drift out wide on exit; the outside kerb can help your traction, but do not exceed track limits. Focus on getting a good exit for the following sequence.

These are taken in 5th gear with just slight lifts. Position the car immediately after turn 4 to take turn 5 cleanly, then flick it left through turn 6. Next, brace for turn 7 with a slight application of brakes. Dont clip the curbs too harshly here — they are flat but can destabilise the rear of your car. Carrying momentum through this section sets up your approach to the famous hairpin.

A slow corner approached downhill. Brake hard, down to 2nd gear, and aim for a late apex. The key is traction on exit — its a very easy place to spin the rears, so short-shifting to 3rd can help you keep your car facing the right way.

sections.heading: Sector 3
sections.body:
This is one of the trickiest corners on the calendar. Youre braking while turning, downhill, into a blind apex. Begin braking early while straight for turn 9, then gradually turn left and reduce speed to 2nd gear for turn 10. Dont lock the unloaded front-left wheel — use a bit more rear brake bias or trail-brake carefully. Getting this right gives a long straight to recover or attack.

A flowing corner taken in 4th gear. Brake gently and turn in confidently, keeping the vehicle centred on the road without climbing the kerb. Exit wide and use the track width to carry momentum. The rear may step out on throttle — keep it clean and avoid excess wheelspin.

Stay full throttle if grip allows, or lift slightly. This sets up turn 13, so positioning is crucial here. Brake hard down to 3rd gear. Apex late and get on the throttle early to carry speed into the next DRS zone. Use all the exit kerbs to maximise your drive onto the penultimate straight.

Brake at the 100m board, down to 2nd or 3rd gear. You will rotate through turn 14 and immediately straighten up for turn 15. The DRS detection occurs just before the final apex, so ensure you are within a second if chasing another car. Get back on throttle early and cleanly, as the long straight starts here.
